Issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
Subject: Claude access lost
Response:
Access to a Claude team workspace is controlled by your organization's admins — only an admin or workspace owner can restore a removed seat, not Anthropic support.

To restore your access, your IT admin needs to:
1. Go to Organization settings > Organization.
2. Find your account.
3. Click the dropdown under Seat Type.
4. Select "Enterprise seat."

Please reach out to your IT admin or workspace owner and ask them to follow those steps. Once they reassign a seat to you, your access will be restored.
Justification: The user is asking for direct restoration of access, but the corpus confirms seat assignment is an admin-controlled action. Grounded on 'Purchase and manage seats on Enterprise plans' (claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/13393991-purchase-and-manage-seats-on-enterprise-plans.md), which details the unassign/reassign process that only an org admin can perform. The route notes also confirm that Anthropic support cannot act on behalf of a non-admin user's workspace. Replied with the steps the admin must take.

Response:

Regarding banning the seller from accepting payments — that falls outside what Visa cardholder support can action directly. Your issuing bank is the right point of contact to escalate merchant concerns through the dispute process.

Issue:
I am facing an blocker while doing compatible check all the criterias are matching other than zoom 
connectivity. Due to which i am unable to take the test. I have done all through my way by 
changing the settings and system configurations but still showing error
Subject: I am facing an blocker while doing compatible check
Response:
The Zoom connectivity check on the compatibility page verifies that the required Zoom domains are not blocked on your network. To resolve this, ensure the following domains are allowlisted in your network or firewall settings:

- *.zoom.us
- *.*.zoom.us
- zoom.us

For the full list of URLs and IP addresses to allowlist, see: https://support.hackerrank.com/articles/6769658535

Also make sure you are using the latest version of Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, or Mozilla Firefox, as Zoom-powered calls are only supported on those browsers.

If you are on a corporate or restricted network, you may need to contact your IT/network administrator to allowlist those domains. For further troubleshooting on test login errors, see: https://candidatesupport.hackerrank.com/articles/2573735554-test-login-errors

If none of these steps resolve the issue, please reach out to HackerRank support directly so they can investigate your specific setup.
Justification: Grounded primarily on the 'Audio and Video Calls in Interviews Powered by Zoom' article (hackerrank/interviews/getting-started/6271433412-audio-and-video-calls-in-interviews-powered-by-zoom.md), which lists the exact Zoom domains to allowlist, and supplemented by the April 2026 Release Notes chunk (hackerrank/general-help/release-notes/4368819843-april-2026-release-notes.md) which references the troubleshooting login errors article. The chunks are topical but do not provide a step-by-step fix for every configuration scenario, so partial escalation note was added.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
Response:
You can update the name on your certificate, but note that it can only be changed once per account. When you update your name, the change applies to all your certificates and cannot be changed again after that.

To update the name on your certificate:

1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click Regenerate Certificate.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.

The platform updates your certificate with the new name.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
Response:
To block Anthropic's crawler (ClaudeBot) from your website, add the following to the robots.txt file in your top-level directory:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please do this for every subdomain that you wish to opt out from.

If you only want to limit (rather than fully block) crawling activity, you can use the Crawl-delay directive instead:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

For more details, see: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler
Justification: Grounded directly on the 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?' article (claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md), which provides verbatim robots.txt instructions for blocking ClaudeBot.

Issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
Subject: Visa card minimum spend
Response:
In the US Virgin Islands, a merchant is permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for Visa credit cards. This is an exception to the general rule — normally, merchants are not permitted to establish a minimum or maximum amount for a Visa transaction, but this exception applies in the USA and US territories, including Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, and Guam, and only for credit cards.

If the merchant is requiring a minimum on a Visa debit card, or is requiring a minimum greater than US$10 on a credit card, that is not permitted. In that case, please notify your Visa card issuer.
Justification: Grounded directly on the 'Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?' chunk (visa/support.md), which explicitly addresses the US$10 minimum exception in US territories including the US Virgin Islands. The derived_from_top_chunk label 'support' was overridden in favor of 'merchant_support' from the canonical label set, as the content is clearly about merchant acceptance rules.
